items.title: GSF 2.0: Eight Malaysians detained by Israeli forces in Gaza-bound flotilla interception
items.summary: SEPANG, May 18 — The Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) has confirmed that eight Malaysians participating in the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 mission were detained by the Israeli Forces as of 6pm Malaysian time today after the vessels they were travelling on were intercepted while en route to deliver humanitarian aid to Gaza. Activist Zainal Rashid Ahmad was detained at 5pm while sailing aboard the vessel Amanda. Sani Araby said 11 flotilla vessels had been intercepted, approached and subjected to direct interference by Israeli forces, namely Cactus, Zio Faster, Holy Blue, Ferluto, Kyriakos, Amanda, Blue Toys, Barbaris, Isobella, Josef and Tenaz (Freedom Flotilla).

items.title: Thai court accepts petition challenging government's US$12bil borrowing decree
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand's Constitutional Court on Monday accepted a petition from the parliamentary opposition challenging the legality of a government emergency decree to borrow 400 billion baht (US$12.26 billion) to support the economy amid the fallout from the Middle East conflict. The court in a statement said it has instructed the government to submit explanatory documents within seven days. Finance Minister Ekniti Nitithanprapas earlier said the decree remains in effect and can still move forward.

items.title: Kedah BN weighs going solo too
items.summary: SIK: Kedah Barisan Nasional (BN) has not ruled out the possibility of contesting solo in the next election, following Johor BN's recent move ahead of its own state polls. Mahdzir added that Johor BN came to its decision to contest solo in all 56 state seats after weighing various factors. On Saturday, Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced that the coalition would field candidates in all 56 state seats in the upcoming state election, saying the decision was finalised after discussions with state Umno and BN grassroots leaders.

items.title: Zara Qairina inquest hears emotional mother-daughter audio recordings
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 18 — The Coroner’s Court here today heard a glimpse of a daughter’s love and distress, as six of 42 audio recordings between the late Zara Qairina Mahathir and her mother, Noraidah Lamat, were played in court. I love you,” Zara Qairina was heard saying, to which Noraidah tenderly replied, “I love you, Ra. “I only extracted these conversation recordings from WhatsApp.

items.title: Jealousy, unrequited love believed motive in woman's stabbing case
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Jealousy and unrequited love are believed to have been the motives behind a man who allegedly stabbed a woman in the neck while inside a car in front of a restaurant along Jalan Titi Gajah near here last night. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said, however, that investigations were ongoing to establish the exact motive in the incident involving a 32-year-old suspect and a 35-year-old victim. The female victim sustained a neck injury, believed to have been caused by a sharp object, and was found inside the car covered in blood.

items.title: 99 Speed Mart posts higher net profit of RM188.56mil in Q1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: 99 Speed Mart Retail Holdings Bhd posted a higher net profit of RM188.56 million in the first quarter of its financial year ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 2026), compared with RM144.90 million in the same period last year. The revenue jumped to RM3.07 million in Q1 2026 compared to RM2.61 million in Q1 2025, attributed to the continued expansion of its outlet network, with a net addition of 253 outlets year-on-year, bringing the total outlet count to 3,086 as of March 31, 2026. "Revenue growth was also supported by the implementation of earlier operating hours nationwide.

items.title: Iran announces new body to manage Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's top security body announced on Monday the formation of a new body to manage the Strait of Hormuz, which Teheran has effectively closed and wants to charge ships to traverse. On its official X account, the Supreme National Security Council shared a post for the Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) saying it would provide "real-time updates on the #Hormuz_Strait operations and latest developments." Iran has largely blocked shipping through the vital strait since the outbreak of war with the United States and Israel on Feb 28.

items.title: Kedah cop linked to Langkawi drug syndicate transferred amid probe
items.summary: ALOR STAR: A police officer believed to be the mastermind, known as "Mr A", in a drug trafficking network operating around Langkawi has been immediately transferred to another department, Kedah police confirmed. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the transfer was not intended to protect the officer, but was carried out as investigations were still ongoing. "We will take action even if the suspect is a member of the police force," he said at a press conference at the Kedah police headquarters today.

items.title: Pos Malaysia's net loss narrows to RM19.5mil in Q1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pos Malaysia Bhd's net loss narrowed to RM19.54 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, from a net loss of RM41.52 million in the same period last year, supported by stronger contributions across its business segments. The postal and parcel services provider saw revenue growth of 7.3 per cent to RM501.35 million from RM467.06 million, driven mainly by its postal segment. According to its Bursa Malaysia filing, the revenue from the postal segment rose 1.44 per cent to RM283.15 million from RM279.11 million, supported by higher contributions from the courier business, although this was partially offset by lower international mail revenue.

items.title: KLK's net profit jumps 91pct in Q2 boost by manufacturing segment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd's (KLK) net profit jumped 90.6 per cent to RM294.05 million in the second quarter ended Mar 31, 2026 versus RM154.27 million in the corresponding period last year due to improved results from the manufacturing segment. Revenue for the quarter under review climbed to RM6.55 billion from RM6.34 billion in the same quarter last year. KLK said the improved performance was also attributable to land sales amounting to RM126.8 million.

items.title: Gaza-bound flotilla says Israeli forces intercepting fleet near Cyprus
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israeli forces were intercepting a Gaza-bound aid flotilla on Monday after it set sail from Turkey last week, with organisers saying one of the vessels was stopped west of Cyprus. "Military vessels are currently intercepting our fleet and IDF forces are currently boarding the first of our boats in broad daylight," the Global Sumud Flotilla posted on X. On Monday, Israel had vowed to block the vessels.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 18, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed lower on Monday, as selling pressure spread across key index-heavy stocks. The benchmark index ended the session at 1,727 points, with sentiment weighed down by rising geopolitical uncertainty linked to the Iran conflict. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: TVET-AI programme to be rolled out in five SBP schools this year, says MoE
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Education Ministry (MoE) has formalised a strategic collaboration through a memorandum of understanding with Multimedia University and Huawei Technologies (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd to strengthen Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) in artificial intelligence (AI) at fully residential schools. The ministry said the TVET-AI initiative would be implemented this year in five selected fully residential schools before being expanded nationwide. The schools involved are Sekolah Menengah (SM) Sains Banting in Selangor, SM Sains Setiu in Terengganu, SM Sains Alor Gajah in Melaka, SM Sains Kuching in Sarawak, and SM Sains Tun Syed Sheh Shahabudin in Penang.

items.title: Sunway Construction's Q1 net profit up 56pct to RM118.41mil
items.summary: Its managing director Liew Kok Wing said the group's healthy outstanding order book, coupled with robust order book replenishment, continues to provide strong visibility for its earnings growth trajectory. "In Q1 2026, the group secured RM3.59 billion worth of new orders, achieving more than 50 per cent of its 2026 order book replenishment target," he said in a separate filing. On prospects, he said the group continues to strengthen its position in Malaysia's key growth sectors, particularly the advanced technology facilities (ATF) segment, while maintaining a diversified construction order book profile across various sectors, including Sunway Group's projects.

items.title: Aeon posts higher net profit of RM83.27mil in Q1
items.summary: This increase was driven by heightened festive shopping activity, supported by effective cost management and enhanced operational discipline. Revenue inched down to RM1.242 billion in Q1 FY26, from RM1.244 billion in Q1 FY25, driven by effective cost management and improved operational efficiency. Aeon managing director Tsugutoshi Seko said the company remain focused on strengthening its operational fundamentals and advancing key growth drivers.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues severe weather alert for Klang Valley, Johor, East Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 18 — Malaysia's Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) issued a thunderstorm warning this evening covering wide swaths of Peninsular Malaysia and East Malaysia, cautioning residents to expect heavy rain, strong winds and lightning through 10.00 pm. The alert, released at 6.25 pm, places the federal territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya under the warning, along with seven districts in Selangor: Kuala Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang. In East Malaysia, the warning extends to multiple areas in Sarawak, including Sri Aman, parts of Kapit (Song and Bukit Mabong), Bintulu, and several localities in Miri and Limbang.

items.title: Capital A net profit drops 96pct in Q1 due to forex loss
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Capital A Bhd logged a lower net profit of RM22.45 million for the first quarter (Q1) ended March 31, 2026, down 96.7 per cent compared with RM689.57 million in the same period a year earlier. The decrease was mainly due to a RM2 million foreign exchange loss in the first quarter, compared with a RM21 million foreign exchange gain in the corresponding quarter of 2025. Despite the lower earnings, Capital A reported higher revenue, rising 84.9 per cent to RM766.59 million from RM414.52 million in the corresponding quarter, according to its Bursa Malaysia filing.

items.title: Farmiera posts RM3mil in net profit for Q1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Poultry producer and distributor Farmiera Bhd logged a net profit of RM3.07 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026. The company recorded revenue of RM167.20 million for the quarter, comprising RM87.17 million from poultry farming and RM80.03 million from poultry processing, according to its Bursa Malaysia filing. It posted a profit before tax of RM4.49 million and a profit after tax of RM3.07 million for the period.

items.title: Showdowns and spycraft on Trump-Xi summit sidelines
items.summary: From a standoff over a US Secret Service agent's weapon to a US staffer trampled during a melee, tensions bubbled to the surface throughout Trump's visit to Beijing. Two incidents made headlines, including when Chinese officials blocked US officials and journalists in a room during Trump and Xi's visit to the Temple of Heaven. "US press, we are going," a US official said.

items.title: KPDN busts diesel syndicate abusing fleet cards in Penang raid
items.summary: Penang KPDN director S. Jegan said initial inspections at the premises uncovered activities involving the misappropriation of subsidised diesel. He said authorities also seized 13 fleet cards believed to have been used in the operation. Jegan said seven local men were detained to assist investigations.

items.title: Gaza aid flotilla says Israeli forces intercepted 10 boats, contact lost with 23 vessels
items.summary: ISTANBUL: The organisers of a flotilla of aid vessels bound for Gaza said on Monday that Israeli forces had intercepted 10 of their boats and that contact had been lost with a total 23 vessels in the eastern Mediterranean. Earlier on Monday, Israel's foreign ministry had said on X that it "will not allow any breach of the lawful naval blockade on Gaza." Live video showed military vessels approaching the vessels on Monday.

items.title: Ampang PKR leader urges Rodziah to quit seat over Rafizi-linked event
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An Ampang PKR divisional leader has called on Ampang MP Rodziah Ismail to vacate her parliamentary seat and resign from the party following her attendance at an event linked to former PKR deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli in Petaling Jaya yesterday. Ampang PKR deputy chief Daing Reduan Bachok said Rodziah's recent actions had triggered concern among grassroots members and party supporters. "Ampang PKR wishes to stress that the grassroots here remain firmly behind the party president (Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim).

items.title: WCT wins RM152.68mil Taiwan highway interchange contract
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: WCT Holdings Bhd has secured a RM152.68 million contract to undertake construction works for a highway interchange project in Taiwan. It said the contract was awarded to WCT Berhad Taiwan Branch, comprising construction works between the Gaoke Interchange and Gangshan Interchange under the "National Highway No. "The works under the contract involve road works, bridge structural works and drainage works," said the engineering, construction and property development company.

items.title: Taiwan says would 'welcome' its president speaking to Trump
items.summary: TAIPEI: Taiwan would "welcome" an opportunity for its leader to speak to US President Donald Trump after he raised the possibility, a senior Taiwanese diplomat said Monday. Trump told reporters on Friday that he had to speak to the man "running Taiwan" – an apparent reference to President Lai Ching-te – about arms sales. I'm going to see," Trump said in response to a question about whether he would go ahead with arms sales to Taiwan.

items.title: Cuckoo's earnings drops in Q1 on softer demand
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Cuckoo International (MAL) Bhd posted lower net profit in the first quarter ended Mar 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26) due to softer consumer demand. Earnings for the quarter under review slipped 10.1 per cent year-on-year to RM25.03 million from RM27.86 million a year ago. Revenue dropped 23.1 per cent to RM227.83 million in Q1 FY26 from RM296.28 milliom in the same period last year as the company saw lower units sold in the quarter.

items.title: Police suspect Kedah woman stabbed in the neck for rejecting attacker’s romantic interest
items.summary: Kedah Police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said police were, however, still conducting detailed investigations to determine the main motive behind the incident involving the 32-year-old suspect and the 35-year-old victim. “Police received a call regarding the incident at 9.24 pm, informing of a fight in front of a restaurant. The female victim sustained injuries to the neck, believed to have been caused by a sharp object, and was found lying inside the car, covered in blood.

items.title: Oil prices stay elevated amid geopolitical risk premium, says MARC Ratings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Global oil prices are holding at elevated levels due to ongoing geopolitical tensions, even though supply still exceeds demand in the market, according to Malaysian Rating Corporation Bhd (MARC Ratings). He said this could help sustain oil demand at elevated levels even though the global market is fundamentally in a state of oversupply. He said that while the global oil market remains structurally oversupplied, prices are being kept elevated by an ongoing geopolitical risk premium.

items.title: Malaysia, India to deepen ties under Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia will continue to strengthen its close ties with India for mutual benefit, regional stability and more inclusive prosperity for both nations, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar said the matter was discussed during a farewell courtesy call by India's High Commissioner to Malaysia, B.N. The prime minister said areas of cooperation included trade, digitalisation, artificial intelligence (AI), food security, education and people-to-people exchanges.

items.title: Oil prices stay elevated amid geopolitical risk premium, says MARC Ratings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Global oil prices are holding at elevated levels due to ongoing geopolitical tensions, even though supply still exceeds demand in the market, according to Malaysian Rating Corporation Bhd (MARC Ratings). He said this could help sustain oil demand at elevated levels even though the global market is fundamentally in a state of oversupply. He said that while the global oil market remains structurally oversupplied, prices are being kept elevated by an ongoing geopolitical risk premium.

items.title: Loan shark runner jailed 11 and a half years for Molotov attack in Batu Pahat
items.summary: the Sessions Court judge, Osman Affendi Mohd Shalleh, asked before sentencing a man to 6 years' jail for acting as a runner for an illegal moneylender, or 'loan shark', in a case involving the firebombing of a senior citizen's home last month. The accused, Mohamed Hanaffi Ahmad, 32, who had been sentenced to 66 months' imprisonment by the Segamat Sessions Court on April 30, pleaded guilty to the charge under Section 436 of the Penal Code. Police also found a video on the wife's mobile phone showing the accused throwing the Molotov cocktail at the victim's rented house.

items.title: ‘Spare a thought for voters’: Azalina reminds elected reps of their five-year commitment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 18 — Members of Parliament and elected representatives have been reminded to respect the people’s mandate by completing their full five-year term of service before making any personal political decisions, including vacating seats or switching parties. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said deliberately vacating a parliamentary seat would only disadvantage the people who had fulfilled their responsibility by voting. She said this in response to situations or speculation involving actions by certain politicians reportedly intending to vacate their parliamentary seats.

items.title: Flare-wielding convoy: Police hunt group after viral video shows Bukit Bintang stunt
items.summary: Dang Wangi OCPD ACP Sazalee Adam said the reckless act posed a significant risk to public safety in the popular tourist hotspot. “This could pose a danger to public safety, disrupt public order, and affect the peace of residents and traders in the area,” he told a press conference today. Police are urging members of the public with any information to contact the Dang Wangi police headquarters at 03-2600 2222 or visit their nearest police station.

items.title: 'Pity the voters': Azalina says lawmakers shouldn't quit seats before term ends
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Members of parliament should not arbitrarily vacate their seats before their terms have ended, said Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said. The minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) said that while quitting is a democratic right, elected representatives bear a fundamental responsibility to their constituents to voice their concerns in the Dewan Rakyat. "Pity the people, the voters in Pandan (constituency).

items.title: EcoWorld shareholding shift sees entry of Syed Mokhtar as substantial Investor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Eco World Development Group Bhd has announced a change in its substantial shareholder structure following the transfer of all of Datuk Leong Kok Wah's shares in Syabas Tropikal Sdn Bhd to Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Albukhary. Following the transaction, Leong ceased to be a substantial shareholder, while Syed Mokhtar emerged as a new substantial shareholder via indirect interest. Syabas Tropikal wholly owns Sinarmas Harta Sdn Bhd, which in turn holds 969.9 million EcoWorld shares, or about 30.1 percent of EcoWorld's issued share capital.

items.title: Expired road tax: RTD nets Rolls-Royce, Bentley and more in KL operation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Road Transport Department (RTD) has impounded 41 luxury vehicles under "Ops Luxury" conducted in the Federal Territory from January to mid-May this year. Kuala Lumpur RTD director Hamidi Adam said 11 vehicles remained in custody, while 30 cases had been resolved following enforcement action against owners. "Ops Luxury is carried out continuously to identify luxury vehicle owners who fail to comply with road regulations despite owning high-value vehicles.

items.title: Boat carrying migrants sinks off Selangor coast; 16 rescued, 3 missing
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) in Selangor has launched a search and rescue operation for three missing migrants after a "pom-pom" boat carrying 19 people reportedly sprang a leak and sank 2.1 nautical miles southwest of Sungai Apong in Sabak Bernam on Saturday. Selangor MMEA director Captain Abdul Muhaimin Muhammad Salleh said the agency received an emergency call from a crew member at 4.45am. Abdul Muhaimin said the maritime vessel Bagan Datuk was also deployed to strengthen early intervention efforts within the emergency zone.

items.title: Malaysian Army eases age, height and BMI requirements to boost new recruit numbers
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, May 18 — The Malaysian Army (TDM) has introduced improvements to its recruitment requirements for young soldiers to provide wider opportunities for the public to join military service. Army chief General Datuk Azhan Md Othman said the improvements include raising the entry age limit from 25 to 30, as well as allowing applicants who wear glasses to apply. He said the minimum height requirement for male candidates has also been lowered to 1.57 metres from 1.62 metres previously, while the Body Mass Index (BMI) limit has been raised to 28 from 26.9.

items.title: DPM Fadillah urges stronger R&D in energy storage as Santong BESS project launches in Dungun
items.summary: DUNGUN, May 18 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof has called for greater emphasis on research and development (R&D) in energy storage technology following the launch of the 100MW/400MWh Santong Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) project developed by Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB). Fadillah, who is also the Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation, said the move was crucial to reduce Malaysia’s dependence on imported batteries while building domestic capabilities in the production and development of energy-related technologies. He added that Malaysia’s energy transition efforts must address three key elements of the energy trilemma, beginning with energy security to ensure a stable and sufficient supply amid global uncertainties.

items.title: Fishing vessel owners in Labuan file police report over arson after two lorries damaged, losses exceed RM100,000
items.summary: LABUAN, May 18 — The Labuan Commercial Fishing Vessel Owners’ Association has lodged a police report following a series of suspected arson and vandalism incidents involving two lorries, with losses estimated at more than RM100,000. He said the association had offered an RM10,000 reward to anyone who could provide credible information to assist the police investigation. Chi said the first incident occurred on May 13 at about 9 pm at Jalan Ujung Pasir near the old fisheries jetty, where the lorry was parked.

items.title: District police chief in Sarawak under probe for allegedly harassing female officer
items.summary: KUCHING, May 18 — A District Police Chief (KPD) in Sarawak is currently under investigation over allegations of sexually harassing a female officer. Sarawak Police Commissioner Datuk Mohamad Zainal Abdullah confirmed the matter, stating that further investigations were underway to determine the veracity of the report. Earlier, the victim, who is stationed at the same District Police Headquarters (IPD), lodged a police report claiming she was harassed by the senior officer.

items.title: Selangor mulls PPR rental hike to boost maintenance and facilities, says state exco
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 18 — The Selangor State Government is considering raising the rental rates of the People’s Housing Project (PPR) in stages as part of efforts to improve infrastructure maintenance within the housing scheme. State Housing and Culture Committee chairman Datuk Borhan Aman Shah said the move followed the takeover of PPR management by Perumahan dan Hartanah Selangor Sdn Bhd (PHSSB) under an agreement between the Housing and Local Government Ministry (KPKT) and the state government. Also present were KPKT secretary-general Datuk Dr M. Noor Azman Taib and Selangor state secretary Datuk Dr Ahmad Fadzli Ahmad Tajuddin.

items.title: Dewan Rakyat Speaker’s Office confirms receiving Rafizi, Nik Nazmi’s resignation letters
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 18 — The Dewan Rakyat Speaker’s Office has confirmed it has received the official resignation letters from Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, formally notifying Parliament of the vacancies for the two federal seats. According to The Star , an official from the Speaker’s Office confirmed receipt of the notices today in response to media queries. The confirmation follows an earlier joint statement by the two former lawmakers, in which they announced they had submitted their letters to Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul.

items.title: Court of Appeal upholds 30‑year jail term for ex‑lecturer for murder, firing gun during bank robbery in Selangor
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 18 — The Court of Appeal today upheld the 30-year prison sentence imposed on a former college lecturer for murder and discharging a firearm during a bank robbery four years ago. Deputy public prosecutor Mohd Zain Ibrahim had sought either the death penalty or 40 years’ imprisonment for Chai Kong Wee @ Chia Kong Wee on the murder charge. Alternatively, he asked that the 30-year sentences imposed for each charge run consecutively rather than concurrently.

items.title: Prosecution submits 45 documents to defence in ex‑army chief and wife’s money‑laundering case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 18 — A total of 45 documents, including recorded conversations and bank account statements, have been handed over to the defence in the money laundering case involving former Army Chief Tan Sri Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan and his wife, Salwani Anuar. On January 22, Muhammad Hafizuddeain, 58, was charged at the Sessions Court here on four counts of receiving proceeds from illegal activities totalling RM969,000, RM474,850, RM488,550 and RM190,000, which were deposited into three bank accounts belonging to him. On January 23, Muhammad Hafizuddeain was charged at the Shah Alam Sessions Court on two counts of receiving proceeds from unlawful activities, namely RM115,000 and RM30,000 deposited into his bank account at a bank branch in Ampang Point, Ampang, between February 2, 2024 and May 19, 2025.

items.title: Semiconductor industry in Malaysia remains stable despite conflict in Middle East
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 18 — The semiconductor industry in the country, including in Penang, remains stable despite the prolonged conflict in Middle East, said Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan He said the conflict, which has entered its third month, has indeed posed challenges, especially rising global oil prices and the supply of several critical materials for the semiconductor industry. He said there are several important raw materials in the semiconductor industry that had previously faced supply pressures following instability in the Middle East. However, he said the local supply chains have sought new sources to replace existing supplies.

items.title: Malaysia expects positive GDP growth, backed by investment inflow
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 18 — Malaysia is expecting a positive Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth as investments inflow into the country remain strong. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the first quarter of this year, the country’s GDP remained strong at 5.4 per cent. “Domestic direct investment (DDI) within the country also remains strong, while public spending is still robust,” he added.

items.title: Man pleads not guilty to drink driving charge in crash that seriously injured p‑hailing rider in Klang
items.summary: KLANG, May 18 — A part-time event planning assistant pleaded not guilty at the Magistrates’ Court here today to a charge of driving under the influence of alcohol, causing serious injury to a p-hailing delivery rider last week. Low Kean Peng, 35, is accused of driving a vehicle with a blood alcohol level of 196 mg per 100 ml of sample. However, the accused’s lawyer, Ethan W.H.

items.title: Video of Zara Qairina cycling was authentic and unaltered, analyst tells Coroner’s Court
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 18 — The Coroner’s Court here was told today that a video recording showing Zara Qairina Mahathir riding a bicycle on July 12 last year was authentic and had not been altered in any way. Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) Computer Crime Investigation Unit analyst ASP Mohamad Zaidi Abu Hassan said technical analysis conducted on the recording confirmed that the video was original. In addition, Mohamad Zaidi said WhatsApp conversations between July 12 and 13 last year extracted from a mobile phone labelled “WF11” comprised 3,285 pages of material.

items.title: Gagasan Rakyat revamp vital for leadership renewal and continuity, says Masidi
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 18 — Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah’s (Gagasan Rakyat) restructuring is an important step to ensure the continuity and renewal of the party’s leadership for the future, said deputy president Datuk Seri Panglima Masidi Manjun. According to him, the current leadership must accept the reality that a leadership transition process is necessary to ensure the party remains relevant and capable of further growth. He said leaders must also be prepared to give opportunities to more capable individuals for the sake of strengthening the party.

items.title: P-hailing rider dies after collision with car exiting junction in Kota Marudu
items.summary: KOTA MARUDU, May 18 — A 28-year-old p-hailing rider died after a motorcycle crash with a Perodua Kancil at Jalan Simpang Kampung Bunsadan yesterday. Mohd Shahriman Basman suffered severe head injuries after the car, driven by a 68-year-old woman, suddenly exited a junction into his path. He died four hours later at Kota Marudu Hospital.

items.title: Making it official: Rafizi, Nik Nazmi confirm vacating Pandan and Setiawangsa federal seats, effective today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 18 — Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad have resigned as members of Parliament of Pandan and Setiawangsa respectively, effective today. “With this, we vacate the parliamentary seats of Pandan and Setiawangsa following our intention to explore a new political agenda,” the statement read. Rafizi and Nik Nazmi also confirmed that they will contest again in the Pandan and Setiawangsa parliamentary seats under the Bersama platform in the upcoming election.

items.title: Selangor police bust four drug syndicates, seize RM17.8m in narcotics
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 18 — Police have busted four drug trafficking syndicates operating around Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) and Ampang, with the seizure of 251.8kg of marijuana buds and syabu worth RM17.8 million in separate raids recently. Selangor Police Chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said six foreign nationals were arrested on May 6, 9 and 10 after following the discovery of suspicious luggage during routine baggage scanning at KLIA Terminal 1. “The six foreign nationals comprising three men and three women aged between 17 and 31 were arrested around the KLIA Terminal 1.

items.title: Only Singapore and Vietnam still carry out executions in SE Asia, Amnesty says; five Malaysians among 17 put to death
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 18 — Singapore and Vietnam remain the only South-east Asian countries to carry out executions, non-governmental human rights organisation Amnesty International Malaysia revealed in its 2025 global report today. Revealing the global death penalty trends, the report highlights a significant rise in executions in Singapore, which nearly doubled from nine in 2024 to 17 in 2025. Amnesty International recorded a total of 138 death sentences in Vietnam, 100 of which were for drug-related offences.

items.title: Bung Moktar’s widow Zizie Izette to know fate over RM2.8m bribery charges on July 20
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 18 — The Sessions Court has set July 20 to deliver its verdict on Datin Seri Zizie Izette Abdul Samad, who faces three corruption charges involving RM2.8 million linked to a RM150 million investment in Public Mutual Berhad unit trusts. Zizie Izette, the widow of the late Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin, former Kinabatangan MP, faces three charges of conspiring with her husband to obtain RM2.8 million in cash bribes. Zizie Izette, 47, is accused of conspiring with Bung Moktar, then non-executive chairman of Felcra Berhad, to obtain RM2.2 million in cash bribes from Madhi Abdul Hamid, a Public Mutual Berhad investment agent.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh seeks MACC briefing this week on 70pc loss of KL flood retention ponds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 18 — Minister Hannah Yeoh said she is seeking a high-level briefing from the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) this week to get a full update on the investigation into the controversial alienation of land at the Batu and Jinjang flood retention ponds. The minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) said her office is arranging the urgent meeting to understand the outcome of the probe. The Segambut MP stated that the briefing will involve all key agencies, including the Federal Territories Department, Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL), and the Federal Territories Land and Mines Office, New Straits Times reported.

items.title: Selangor police nab ex-con debt collector for sodomising teen lured with fake phone package
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 18 — A debt collector with a prior criminal record has been arrested in connection with the confinement, abuse, and sodomy of a 14-year-old boy in Bukit Kemuning earlier this month. Selangor Police Chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar confirmed that the male suspect was arrested in his rented room in Kuala Lumpur on Saturday, May 16. “If the investigation is not yet complete, we will apply for an extension of the remand order,” Shazeli told a press conference at the Selangor Police Contingent Headquarters today.

items.title: Law minister confirms revised AG-PP split Bill to be tabled June 22; hopes politicians defer moves until after vote
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 18 — The revised Bill for the proposed separation of the Attorney General’s (AG) and Public Prosecutor’s (PP) roles will be tabled in the Dewan Rakyat on June 22, Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said today. Azalina expressed hope that MPs from the federal opposition would support this revised Bill, as a two-thirds majority (148 out of 222 MPs in the Dewan Rakyat) would be needed for this proposed law change. I hope.

items.title: Amnesty report reveals sharp decline in Malaysia’s death sentences but gaps remain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 18 — Human rights non-governmental organisation Amnesty International Malaysia today unveiled its 2025 report, revealing a notable decrease in the implementation of death sentences in the country. According to the report, Malaysia has made significant progress in reducing the use of the death penalty over the past year Presenting the reports, Amnesty International Malaysia spokesperson Divya Shesshsan Balakrishnan stated that the number of new death sentences has continued to fall since the landmark abolition of the mandatory death penalty in 2023, marking a turning point in the nation’s criminal justice system. Notably, no new death sentences were reported in the states of Sabah and Sarawak last year.

items.title: Kelantan murder cases up 100 pc in first four months of 2026; police blame religious decay
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 18 — Kelantan police have voiced concern over a sharp rise in murder cases, which increased by over 100 per cent between January and April this year compared with the same period last year. State police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said five murder cases have been recorded to date, up from just one case last year. Meanwhile, Mohd Yusoff said the state police had successfully solved a murder case involving a 19-year-old disabled man in Kampung Chap Bekelam, Bachok, on April 14.

items.title: King of Malaysia appoints 15 new envoys to Japan, UK, Singapore, other countries
items.summary: Also receiving their instruments are Malaysian Ambassador to Austria Datuk Fenny Nuli, Malaysian High Commissioner to New Zealand Abu Bakar Mamat, Malaysian Ambassador to Laos Norli Muadza Mohd Noor and Malaysian Ambassador to Oman Ahmad Kamrizamil Mohd Riza. Others are Malaysian Ambassador to Ireland Sharon Ho Swee Peng, Malaysian Ambassador to Finland Datuk S. S. Amarjit Singh, Malaysian Ambassador to Timor-Leste Fairuz Adli Mohd Rozali and Malaysian Ambassador to Turkiye Adlan Mohd Shaffieq. Suresh Kumar, Malaysian Ambassador to Lebanon Anwar Udzir, Malaysian Ambassador to Uzbekistan Rafedah Abdul Aziz and Malaysian Ambassador to Azerbaijan Aznifah Isnariah Abdul Ghani.
